forecast_failure.interpretation: Binary discrimination remained above chance, but expected-value ranking was directionally wrong and every reported top tail lost after the frozen execution costs. AUC therefore did not establish an executable edge.

policy_failure.interpretation: Two candidates had positive totals, but both failed the precommitted drawdown, positive-day, and worst-trade controls. Repeated threshold traces were almost entirely long and concentrated in few active days.

supported_conclusions: The frozen Round 30 model did not generalize to the Round 31 confirmation period.
Independent side-specific expected-value heads permitted severe long/short calibration drift.
Profitability classification alone was insufficient to rank trades by realized after-cost value.
Leverage would have magnified the observed drawdown and tail loss and was correctly withheld.
causal_hypotheses_not_yet_proven: Parameter sharing over direction-canonical action rows may reduce side-head drift.
A separate signed-advantage model may improve side selection without confusing it with the decision to trade.
Training and selecting directly on adverse-stress net outcomes may align the model with the policy objective better than fitting base outcomes and screening under stress.
